experience.title: Machine Learning Engineer
bullets: Leading development and release of multiple new full-length ML pipelines, including custom data annotation tools, model architectures, and post-processing steps, aimed at detecting the boundaries of materials, roadways, curbs, and paint lines in aerial images
Designed two novel model architectures with PyTorch leveraging semi-supervised and multi-task learning, both resulting in more than a 20% improvement over previous strategies
Developing extensive tooling for ground-truth data creation using C++, PyQt, and SQL to transform dozens of drone-captured orthomosaics into usable labeled data for computer vision models
Developed modular plugin system for a point cloud visualization software using C++ and ZeroMQ
Created HTTP server, API, and web pages to automate and visualize ML model training, inference, and evaluation

experience.title: Computer Engineering Intern
bullets: Developed physical verification rules in SVRF and designed transistor-level validation cells for unit testing

experience.title: Machine Learning Research Assistant
bullets: Used PyTorch to develop a novel training approach for medical image segmentation models, shown to improve over baselines by up to 32.4% across multiple metrics and datasets
Papers published in ISBI 2024 and MELBA, poster presented at CCS 2023, received UK research fellowship

experience.title: Engineering and Technology Intern
bullets: Created $33,000 in annual savings and reduced report creation time by 90% by building automation tools with VBA

publications.name: PolyCL: Context-Aware Contrastive Learning for Image Segmentation
bullets: Novel contrastive learning strategy for medical image segmentation models, shown to significantly improve over baselines
Published at ISBI 2024, First author

publications.name: SkelPy: A graphic user interface–based approach for skeletonizing fungal networks
bullets: Fully open source and modular skeletonization tool for biology researchers analyzing fungal structures at scale
Published in Applications in Plant Sciences, Primary developer on project

publications.name: Development of a Modular Current-Mode NaI(Tl) Detector Array for Parity Odd (n,γ) Cross Section Measurements
bullets: A detector array, deployed on a particle accelerator at the J-PARC laboratory, for which I built the data acquision system's software and firmware

publications.name: Domain and Task-Focused Example Selection for Data-Efficient Contrastive Medical Image Segmentation
bullets: An expansion of my PolyCL contrastive learning framework to more datasets and comparisons
Published in MELBA

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science and Mathematics
bullets: Lewis Honors College, Competitive Programming Team (placed 1st in Kentucky at ICPC Regionals 2024), Undergraduate Science Journal Club
Department of Computer Science Award for Outstanding Academic Achievement, Dean's List for all 8 semesters
Undergraduate teaching assistant for classes covering Linux, intermediate C++, and UI development

projects.name: Context-aware Multitasking for Medical Image Segmentation
bullets: Novel multi-tasking framework that combines segmentation and classification for medical images
Shows significant improvement over baseline and pre-trained models in Dice score and Haussdorf distance

projects.name: Volt Engine
bullets: Vulkan rendering engine written in C++/HLSL, implements Blinn-Phong lighting with multiple sources
Allows for modifiable instance data (textures, transforms, etc.), adding/removing/modifying objects at runtime, and text rendering

projects.name: Shoulder-Mounted Space Program
bullets: A fully featured game built in Unity, released in December 2025, that features a custom realistic gravity simulation
Successfully coordinated a small team of artists, playtesters, and a composer to get the game ready for release
